text: The surgeon elevates the liver to inspect the stomach for gastric perforation, finding it intact. The gallbladder is lifted, revealing a large ulcer on the lateral wall of the duodenum with food particles present. The surgeon decides on primary closure using a 2-0 V-lock suture, running it to close the ulcer with minimal tension on the duodenal wall.

The surgeon uses scissors to divide adhesions and free a vascularized tongue of omentum, creating an omental patch. The patch is sutured in place with interrupted silk sutures, laying smoothly over the closure without tension. The surgical field shows no bleeding or leaks.
